vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or completely desiccated. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable biological presence, though it is in a state of severe decline.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot is empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Critical Decline (Pothos leaf visible, but chlorotic/wilting)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No visible silver guest foliage)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot. No signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains only debris/soil. No mint present.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen showing severe turgor loss and chlorosis.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells in P3; presence of a small electronic component (IC/chip) on the desk surface near the pots.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows P3 with a healthy leaf and rabbit anchor. P1 is empty.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Introduction of a small electronic component (IC) on the desk surface. P3 leaf begins to show signs of drooping.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Progressive loss of light and visibility; P3 leaf appears to be collapsing or losing structural integrity.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal collapse. The lack of light and potential nutrient/water deficiency has led to the loss of all specimens except for a single, struggling Pothos leaf. The presence of non-biological debris (eggshells, electronics) suggests an uncontrolled environment.
anomalies: Introduction of electronic component (IC) on desk surface.
Crushed eggshells used as soil amendment/top dressing in P3.
Total absence of expected flora in P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a failing indoor ecosystem. The registry is no longer representative of the physical reality, as three of the four pots are effectively devoid of plant life. The Pothos in P3 is the sole survivor but exhibits clear signs of physiological stress, likely due to inadequate light and poor substrate management. The presence of electronic waste on the desk surface indicates a lack of proper horticultural maintenance.
